color.$description: Semantic roles for the neo-brutalist visual language over the Doctor School brand. Light-first; the .dark overrides live in semantic.dark.json. Brand primary = blue.500 #2D84F2 stays the brand ANCHOR (link accents, icons, tints, focus). White-on-fill AA (ADR-0013 ¬ß7): white on blue.500 is only 3.69:1, so a NORMAL-weight filled action does NOT paint blue.500 ‚Äî it uses the accessible action-fill triad `primary-action` (blue.700, white 8.14:1, resting), `primary-hover`/`primary-pressed` (blue.800, 11.12:1), which keeps a visible resting‚Üíhover delta while clearing AA on every state. The same blue.700 rule governs any text-bearing brand FILL: `primary-surface` (blue.700) is the brand fill for a panel carrying normal-weight white copy. Text tiers: `foreground` ink / `muted-foreground` / `faint` / `muted-2` (neutral 900/600/500/400). Frame: structural `border` is the hard near-black outline (neutral.900), `hairline` (neutral.200) the subtle divider. Status = success green, warning amber (dark ink ‚Äî white fails AA on amber), danger `destructive` red. `header` is the brand chrome band. `tint` is the pale-blue info surface. `elevation`/`elevation-soft` are the theme-aware cast-shadow tones the offset shadows reference.

background.$description: The default page/app surface (neo-brutalist page = flat white). Pair with `foreground` for text.

foreground.$description: Ink ‚Äî the default text and icon colour, used on `background`.

section.$description: A faintly-tinted block surface used to band a section apart from the flat page, without a card border.

calendar-muted.$description: The month-calendar's muted day-cell surface (weekend / out-of-month cells + the legend ¬´–ü—Ä–æ—à—ë–ª / –ø—É—Å—Ç–æ¬ª swatch) ‚Äî canvas-exact (webinars-month.dc.html), deliberately fainter than `section` so the shaded cells whisper next to the card frame. Dedicated to the calendar: `section` keeps banding the week-listing day band.

card.$description: A raised surface (card, popover, panel) sitting on `background`, framed by the hard `border`. Pair with `card-foreground`.

card-foreground.$description: Text and icon colour on a `card` surface.

primary.$description: Brand anchor (blue.500) for link accents, focus, icons and tints. NOT a normal-weight filled action ‚Äî that fails AA; use `primary-action`.

primary-foreground.$description: Text and icon colour ON a primary/action fill (white on the brand button).

primary-action.$description: Resting fill for a normal-weight filled primary button (blue.700 = #114D9E, white 8.14:1). Pairs with `primary-foreground`. Also the AA link-text colour.

primary-hover.$description: Hover fill for a primary action (darker than the resting `primary-action`).

primary-pressed.$description: Pressed/active fill for a primary action.

primary-surface.$description: Brand-coloured panel/surface that carries normal-weight white copy (e.g. the auth brand panel). Pair with `primary-surface-foreground` ‚Äî NOT `primary-foreground`, which repoints to dark ink in `.dark` (where `primary-action` lifts to a light-blue fill) and would render near-invisible on this panel.

primary-surface-foreground.$description: Text and icon colour ON a `primary-surface` fill. White in BOTH themes (the surface stays blue.700 in dark, white 8.14:1) ‚Äî deliberately NOT overridden in semantic.dark.json, unlike `primary-foreground` which pairs with the theme-flipping action fill.

primary-surface-muted.$description: Quiet supporting-copy tier ON a `primary-surface` fill ‚Äî the eyebrow / value-prop / footer under a white headline (e.g. the auth brand panel). A fixed muted white (5.81:1 on blue.700, AA in BOTH themes since the surface is invariant), it REPLACES an element `opacity-*` dim on the sub-copy (#537): an opacity dim reads as translucent and is fragile to recolour, so the quiet tier is a real token, one visible weight below `primary-surface-foreground`. Pair with `bg-primary-surface`.

secondary.$description: Low-emphasis tonal fill (secondary buttons, subtle chips). Pair with `secondary-foreground`.

secondary-foreground.$description: Text and icon colour on a `secondary` fill.

muted.$description: A muted background for subtle fills, hovered rows or disabled tracks.

muted-foreground.$description: De-emphasised text ‚Äî helper text, placeholders, captions, metadata (neutral.600).

faint.$description: Third text tier below `muted-foreground` ‚Äî timestamps, tertiary metadata (neutral.500).

muted-2.$description: Faintest text tier ‚Äî disabled labels, decorative captions (neutral.400). Non-body use only; below the AA body-text threshold by design.

accent.$description: Hover/selected background for interactive list items and ghost buttons. Pair with `accent-foreground`.

accent-foreground.$description: Text and icon colour on an `accent` surface.

tint.$description: Pale-blue informational surface (tint chips, callouts). Pair with `tint-foreground`.

tint-foreground.$description: Text and icon colour on a `tint` surface (blue.700 on blue.100, 6.49:1 AA).

info.$description: Informational accent ‚Äî the border + leading icon of an info callout/alert, painted on the pale `tint` surface. The source's `accent` role: brand blue.500 light ‚Üí blue.300 dark (the theme-aware brand accent, distinct from `primary` which stays blue.500 in dark).

chip-border.$description: Resting border of an unselected filter chip ‚Äî the pale brand-blue outline (blue.200 light ‚Üí blue.300 @ 45% dark) that reads as a quiet, tappable outline before selection. Source `chipBorder`.

header.$description: Brand chrome band (top nav / app header) ‚Äî the accessible brand blue.700. Pair with `header-foreground`; white on blue.700 is 8.14:1, clearing WCAG-AA for normal-weight body text as well as large/bold brand or logo (deepened from blue.500 for #713 ‚Äî blue.500's 3.69:1 white met only the large/bold ‚â•3:1 carve-out). Same value in both theme sets ‚Äî the header band does not change with the theme (006 design ¬ß10).

header-foreground.$description: Text, logo and icon colour on the `header` band (white).

header-hairline.$description: On-header muted border ‚Äî the half-strength (white @ 50%) hairline of the header's icon-button family (the canvas `rgba(255,255,255,.5)`); hover raises to full-strength `header-foreground`. Same value in both theme sets ‚Äî the header band does not change with the theme (006 design ¬ß10).

hero.$description: Discovery-hero poster band (the `/webinars` week + month listing heroes) ‚Äî the canvas `headerBg`: brand blue.500 light ‚Üí blue.700 dark (`webinars-listing.dc.html` / `webinars-month.dc.html` line 291). Distinct from `header` (the top-nav chrome, pinned at the #713 AA-deepened blue.700 in both themes): the hero carries only LARGE/BOLD display text in `hero-foreground` (white on blue.500 = 3.69:1, inside the WCAG large-text ‚â•3:1 carve-out) plus `hero-muted` poster-decor small print that is leaf-excluded from the axe gate (#924).

hero-foreground.$description: Display text colour on the `hero` band (white). Large/bold text only on the light blue.500 fill ‚Äî see `hero`.

hero-muted.$description: Dimmed poster ink on the `hero` band ‚Äî the canvas `#D3E8FD` subtitle/tagline tier (blue.100, both themes). Decorative poster small print (`data-testid="poster-decor"`, axe leaf-exclusion #924).

success.$description: Positive-status fill, border or icon. Pair with `success-foreground` (white is large/bold on green.500 = 3.68:1).

success-foreground.$description: Text and icon colour on a `success` fill.

success-text.$description: Success as FOREGROUND TEXT on a neutral surface ‚Äî the inline field success confirmation (`FormMessage success`, source ¬ß07 `‚úì <msg>`). The green mirror of `destructive-text` (#529): the `success` FILL (green.500 #009959) as TEXT is only 3.68:1 on the white card, so the confirmation message rides its own darker green (light green.700 #047857, 5.49:1) while the border + ‚úì-on-tint keep the brand green.500. Dark lifts to the brighter green.400 (same as the `success` fill) for AA on the near-black card. Pair on `background` / `card`, never as a fill foreground.

success-tint.$description: Pale-green success surface behind a success callout/alert ‚Äî the source's success tint. Carries normal `foreground` ink; the `success` border + ‚úì icon carry the semantic. Theme-aware: pale-green light ‚Üí deep-green dark.

warning.$description: Caution-status fill, border or icon (functional amber). Pair with `warning-foreground` ‚Äî white FAILS AA on amber, so the foreground is dark ink.

warning-foreground.$description: Dark ink on a `warning` amber fill (neutral.950, 5.14:1 on amber.500 / 8.99:1 on amber.400 dark).

warning-tint.$description: Pale-amber caution surface behind a warning callout/alert ‚Äî the source's warn tint. Carries normal `foreground` ink; the `warning` border + ‚öÝ icon carry the semantic. Theme-aware: pale-amber light ‚Üí deep-amber dark.

destructive.$description: Error/danger/live fill, border or icon ‚Äî destructive actions, invalid state, live indicators. Pair with `destructive-foreground`.

destructive-foreground.$description: Text and icon colour on a `destructive` fill.

destructive-text.$description: Danger as FOREGROUND TEXT on a neutral surface ‚Äî the inline field error / form error message (`FormMessage` / `FormError`, source ¬ß07 `‚öÝ <msg>`). Split from the `destructive` FILL role (#537): a FILL under white text must be dark enough for AA (red.500 #C81E1E, white 5.74:1), but the SAME red AS TEXT on the near-black dark card is only 3.09:1 ‚Äî so the two roles need different darks. Light red.500 #C81E1E (5.74:1 on the white card); dark red.400 #E15555 (4.75:1 on the dark card) ‚Äî the error message stays legible in both themes while the button fill lifts to the darker AA-safe red. Pair on `background` / `card`, never as a fill foreground.

destructive-tint.$description: Pale-red field/error surface (danger tint) behind an invalid input ‚Äî the source's `dangerTint`. Carries normal `foreground` ink; the destructive border + `‚öÝ` message carry the error. Theme-aware: pale-red light ‚Üí deep-maroon dark.

live.$description: Flat live/danger red (#C81E1E) ‚Äî the fill of a `live` badge ("–≤ —ç—Ñ–∏—Ä–µ") and the border + leading icon of a danger callout/alert. The source's `danger`/`live` constant: INVARIANT across themes (same #C81E1E on the light page and the dark maroon tint). It shares the red.500 primitive with the `destructive` FILL (which is also #C81E1E in both themes since #537), but stays a distinct role: `live` is the non-text danger accent (badge fill / alert border+icon), `destructive` is the interactive danger fill (button / invalid input), and `destructive-text` is the danger error MESSAGE (which alone lifts to the lighter red.400 in dark for text AA). Pair with `live-foreground`.

live-foreground.$description: Text, icon and pulse-dot colour on a `live` fill ‚Äî white in both themes (white on #C81E1E = 5.74:1 AA).

border.$description: Structural border ‚Äî the hard near-black outline (neutral.900) of the neo-brutalist frame (2px). Not the subtle divider; that is `hairline`.

hairline.$description: Subtle 1px divider/rule between rows and list items, where a hard structural `border` would be too heavy.

input.$description: Border colour of form inputs and controls ‚Äî the same hard structural outline as `border`.

ring.$description: The keyboard-focus ring colour (blue.300); the `focus` shadow (`shadow.focus`) is blue.300 @ 50%.

elevation.$description: Primary cast-shadow tone for the hard offset shadows (`shadow.md`/`lg`). Theme-aware: blue.700 light ‚Üí blue.300 dark.

elevation-soft.$description: Softer cast-shadow tone for the small offset shadows (`shadow.sm`/`base`). Theme-aware: blue.200 light ‚Üí blue.600 dark.

space.$description: ¬ß09 ¬´–Ý–∞—Å–∫–ª–∞–¥–∫–∞ –∏ —Ä–∏—Ç–º¬ª semantic spacing ROLES over the ¬ß03 4px scale ‚Äî space is composed by ROLE, not by eye. Each role names WHERE a gap belongs so surfaces stay in rhythm; the source states a small set per role and these tokens carry the canonical (modal) value, with the fuller range noted in the package README. Surfaced as Tailwind spacing utilities via the `--spacing-<role>` @theme namespace: `p-inset`, `gap-controls`, `gap-inline`, `space-y-section`, `px-gutter`, `-mx-gutter` (bleed), ‚Ä¶ The two responsive roles compose with the `layout:` breakpoint variant: STACK is 20px mobile / 28px desktop (`space-y-stack-sm layout:space-y-stack` ‚Äî the mobile value is a recorded owner Stage-B decision, 2026-07-06, superseding the canvas's flush gap:0); GUTTER is a fixed 16px on mobile and the clamp on desktop (owned by the Container primitive). DAY-BAND is 0 ‚Äî the day plate bleeds flush to its cards.

gutter.$description: Container horizontal gutter on desktop (‚â• layout breakpoint): clamp(16px, 4vw, 48px). On mobile the Container uses a fixed 16px gutter instead (edge-to-edge, so day-band/cards bleed cleanly by -16px). Also the negative-margin bleed unit (`-mx-gutter`).

inset.$description: Padding INSIDE cards, panels and buttons (canonical 24px; the source spans 16 ¬∑ 20 ¬∑ 24 ¬∑ 30 ‚Äî reach for `p-4`/`p-5` for tighter insets).

stack.$description: Gap BETWEEN cards in a list ‚Äî 28px on desktop. On mobile the gap narrows to `stack-sm` (20px). Compose responsively: `space-y-stack-sm layout:space-y-stack`.

stack-sm.$description: Mobile card-stack gap ‚Äî 20px, between `controls` (12) and the desktop `stack` (28). Recorded product-owner Stage-B decision (2026-07-06): the owner asked for visible air between mobile cards, superseding the canvas's flush gap:0 (webinars-listing.dc.html mobile cardWrap). The full-bleed flat-card treatment itself is unchanged.

section.$description: Rhythm BETWEEN meaning blocks / sections (canonical 48px; the source spans 44 ¬∑ 48). On mobile the between-day-group rhythm narrows to `section-sm` (32px).

section-sm.$description: Mobile rhythm BETWEEN day groups ‚Äî 32px of air above the next day's band, clearly larger than the intra-day `stack-sm` (20). Recorded product-owner Stage-B decision (2026-07-06, extends the stack-sm override: mobile rhythm = 20 intra-day / 32 between days), superseding the canvas's flush mobile day header (webinars-listing.dc.html dayHeaderStyle margin 0). Compose `mt-section-sm layout:mt-0` on every day-group wrapper EXCEPT the first ‚Äî desktop keeps the full `section` rhythm (48) via the day header's own margins, and the band still sits FLUSH (0) to its own first card (day-band role).

controls.$description: Gap between adjacent controls ‚Äî chips, buttons, fields (canonical 12px; the source spans 8 ¬∑ 10 ¬∑ 12 ‚Äî `gap-2`/`gap-2.5` for tighter clusters).

inline.$description: Gap on the inline axis ‚Äî icon ‚Üî text, label ‚Üî value (canonical 8px; the source spans 6 ¬∑ 8 ‚Äî `gap-1.5` for the tighter pair).

day-band.$description: The day plate sits FLUSH (0) against its cards and bleeds to the container edges ‚Äî see the `-mx-gutter` bleed pattern.

micro-label.$description: Neo-brutalist micro-label / eyebrow: 10px extrabold, wide +.12em tracking, UPPERCASE. Section eyebrows, chip labels, meta tags.
